Task created: feat: cross-model review — chalk doctor warns when the reviewer shares the executor model
Started: feat: cross-model review — chalk doctor warns when the reviewer shares the executor model
Review (pass): feat: cross-model review — chalk doctor warns when the reviewer shares the executor model
Done: feat: cross-model review — chalk doctor warns when the reviewer shares the executor model
Decision: Cross-model adversarial review: chalk doctor warns when the P5 reviewer shares the executor's model
description: self-preference bias (arxiv 2410.21819) + correlated reviewer/generator failure (2604.08401): a same-model adversary self-prefers and shares blind spots. Chalk can't pick the model (BYO executor) so it surfaces the risk via doctor + a cross-family recommendation. M3 of the harness-improvement plan.

title: Task created: feat: harden P7 blindness — doctor fails on git-tracked held-out + pin audit output withholding
Started: feat: harden P7 blindness — doctor fails on git-tracked held-out + pin audit output withholding
Review (pass): feat: harden P7 blindness — doctor fails on git-tracked held-out + pin audit output withholding
Done: feat: harden P7 blindness — doctor fails on git-tracked held-out + pin audit output withholding
Decision: Harden P7 blindness: doctor fails on git-tracked held-out; audit output-withholding pinned
description: ImpossibleBench — isolating tests drops cheating to ~0, leaking them restores it. A worktree is a plain checkout, so a committed held-out file lands in the agent's sandbox. doctor now refuses it. Reviewer noted the withholding test pins the console-leak (inherit) regression but not pipe-without-print (not itself a leak). M1a+M1c.

title: Task created: feat: mutation-testing adequacy gate — block a change whose changed code has surviving mutants (lever 3, rigorous)
Started: feat: mutation-testing adequacy gate — block a change whose changed code has surviving mutants (lever 3, rigorous)

description: reviewer fix→re-review: (med) assert the implementation-file filter excludes tests + .chalk; (low) assert {file} substitution; (low) a tool that can't run is inconclusive, not a false survivor

title: Review (pass): feat: mutation-testing adequacy gate — block a change whose changed code has surviving mutants (lever 3, rigorous)
Done: feat: mutation-testing adequacy gate — block a change whose changed code has surviving mutants (lever 3, rigorous)
Decision: Mutation-testing adequacy gate (rigorous lever 3): lib/mutation.mjs, opt-in via protocol.mutation, wired into work + run driver
description: passing tests != adequate — a benchmark test hit 100% coverage / 4% mutation; surviving mutants in changed code = weak assertions (Meta runs this in prod; Stryker --incremental/cargo-mutants --in-diff make it per-change feasible). Adversarial review caught an unasserted impl-file filter; fixed via amend-spec. Generalizes break-it. M2.

title: Task created: feat: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (close the weaken-after-approval bypass)
Started: feat: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (close the weaken-after-approval bypass)
Review (pass): feat: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (close the weaken-after-approval bypass)
Done: feat: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (close the weaken-after-approval bypass)
Decision: M5: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (marks it 'stale'); review re-runs when the last verdict no longer stands
description: closes the bypass 'get a pass → weaken the locked test via amend-spec → merge on the stale approval'. done/merge P5 checks already require last verdict 'pass'; the fix makes a changed locked test drop that. force-review --why requirement pinned by test. Live-caught: manual 'chalk review' dies on a transient parseVerdict greedy-regex failure (no retry like the pipeline) — robustness follow-up (C1).

title: Task created: feat: size-scaled P7 stringency — held-out set floor grows with code size (SpecBench)
Started: feat: size-scaled P7 stringency — held-out set floor grows with code size (SpecBench)
Review (pass): feat: size-scaled P7 stringency — held-out set floor grows with code size (SpecBench)
Done: feat: size-scaled P7 stringency — held-out set floor grows with code size (SpecBench)
Decision: M4: size-scaled P7 stringency — heldOutFloor(loc, locPerTest) makes the held-out count a floor that grows with code size; audit warns, phase gate refuses (overridable)
description: SpecBench: the held-out-vs-visible gap grows ~28pts per 10x LOC, so a fixed oracle decays. Previously codeSize only triggered staleness; now the bar actually rises with the code — making 'stringency scales with code size' a real mechanism instead of a doc claim. Default locPerTest 2000 keeps tiny projects at floor 0. M4.

title: Task created: fix: robust reviewer verdict parsing — recover the verdict JSON amid reasoning/prose (a transient parse failure blocked a real review)
Started: fix: robust reviewer verdict parsing — recover the verdict JSON amid reasoning/prose (a transient parse failure blocked a real review)
Review (pass): fix: robust reviewer verdict parsing — recover the verdict JSON amid reasoning/prose (a transient parse failure blocked a real review)
Done: fix: robust reviewer verdict parsing — recover the verdict JSON amid reasoning/prose (a transient parse failure blocked a real review)
Decision: C1: robust reviewer verdict parsing — lib/json.mjs balanced-brace scanner (jsonObjects/parseLastJson); review.mjs parseVerdict recovers the last valid verdict object
description: the greedy /{...}/ span grabbed from a stray brace in the reviewer's prose to EOF and failed to parse, blocking a real adversarial review twice this session. Now scans for balanced top-level objects (ignoring braces inside strings) and recovers the operative one. The same fragile pattern remains in retro/feedback/discovery — C2 de-drift follow-up (adopt parseLastJson there).

title: Task created: refactor: adopt robust parseLastJson in retro/feedback/discovery (kill the duplicated greedy JSON regex)
Started: refactor: adopt robust parseLastJson in retro/feedback/discovery (kill the duplicated greedy JSON regex)
Started: refactor: adopt robust parseLastJson in retro/feedback/discovery (kill the duplicated greedy JSON regex)
Review (pass): refactor: adopt robust parseLastJson in retro/feedback/discovery (kill the duplicated greedy JSON regex)
Done: refactor: adopt robust parseLastJson in retro/feedback/discovery (kill the duplicated greedy JSON regex)
Decision: C2: retro/feedback/discovery adopt the robust parseLastJson — duplicated greedy /{...}/ JSON regex removed
description: the same fragile parse that broke the C1 reviewer lived in all three lifecycle agents; they now recover their {lessons,issues}/{issues}/{tasks} payload from prose-wrapped output via lib/json.mjs (shape-predicate per module). Reviewer noted an unbalanced lone-brace corner case — outside contract, and the old regex failed on it too (not a regression).

title: Review (pass): fix: chalk review retries once on a transient reviewer failure (match the pipeline stage)
Done: fix: chalk review retries once on a transient reviewer failure (match the pipeline stage)
Decision: C1-remainder: chalk review retries once on a transient reviewer failure; pipeline + reviewloop pass --no-retry (they own stage-level retry)
description: a manual/direct 'chalk review' died on the first transient flake (a truncated response, then a connection drop) twice this session, unlike the pipeline stage. Now a bounded retry-once — only a second consecutive error is fatal. Reviewer's test-adequacy note addressed via amend-spec, which invalidated the prior pass (M5) and forced a re-review.

title: Task created: fix: chalk release fails loudly on a git-tag error instead of marking work released with no tag
Started: fix: chalk release fails loudly on a git-tag error instead of marking work released with no tag
Review (pass): fix: chalk release fails loudly on a git-tag error instead of marking work released with no tag
Done: fix: chalk release fails loudly on a git-tag error instead of marking work released with no tag
Decision: Release hardening: chalk release tags FIRST; in a git repo a tag failure is fatal before writing CHANGELOG or marking tasks released
description: it used to swallow a failed git tag yet mark work released, shipping onto an untagged version that the next release (seeing them marked) would never re-tag. A non-git project legitimately can't tag → stays a CHANGELOG/pkg-only release. First of the silent-failure autonomous-path hardenings.
